question_text: A motor uses 200 J of electrical energy and does 160 J of useful mechanical work. What happens to the remaining 40 J of energy?

options.label: A
options.text: It is stored in the motor permanently

options.label: B
options.text: It is converted to heat and sound energy (wasted)

options.label: D
options.text: It is converted back to electrical energy

correct_answer: It is converted to heat and sound energy (wasted)
explanation: When a motor converts electrical energy into mechanical work, not all the energy becomes useful work. The 'missing' 40 J is lost as heat (from friction in moving parts) and sound (from vibrations). This is why real motors are less than 100% efficient - some energy is always wasted.
